methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 5 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Innoviz Technologies operates in the Drones & Autonomous sector as a public company (ticker: INVZ). The relevant peer universe consists of five private companies: Joby Aviation, Zipline, Nuro, Archer Aviation, and Elroy Air. No public comparables exist within the sector, creating a constrained comp environment.

Peer-median valuation methodology is not applicable here. The analysis identified zero same-stage peers, zero adjacent-stage peers, and zero similar valuation-tier peers within the Drones & Autonomous universe. While five companies operate in this sector, none satisfied the filtering criteria necessary to establish a reliable peer set of three or more datapoints. An all-sector median approach would introduce material bias given the heterogeneity of business models, maturity profiles, and capital requirements across the five identified private peers.

Given these data constraints, a quantitative triangulation across multiple methods cannot be completed. The analysis lacks sufficient comparable transaction multiples, revenue benchmarks, or margin profiles to derive a credible valuation range. Absent additional private market transaction data, financial performance metrics for the peer set, or secondary market pricing signals, this dataset does not support a narrow valuation conclusion. Further data collection—including detailed financials from the five private peers and any recent funding round valuations—would be required to establish defensible triangulation.
